seo.title: Building Data Extraction with CrewAI + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build data extraction with CrewAI + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building data extraction pipelines with CrewAI enables multi-agent collaboration to parse unstructured documents into structured schemas, leveraging specialized agents for different extraction tasks. Guardrails are essential to prevent agent delegation loops, hallucinated outputs, and task drift that can compromise schema compliance and data accuracy. Vex integration ensures reliable outputs through automated verification at each task step.

architectureOverview.heading: Data Extraction Architecture with CrewAI
architectureOverview.description:
CrewAI orchestrates a crew of agents for data extraction: a Document Analyzer agent preprocesses and segments unstructured text, an Extractor agent identifies key fields using schema-guided prompts, a Validator agent cross-checks completeness and accuracy, and a Formatter agent outputs JSON-compliant results. Each agent executes tasks sequentially or hierarchically, passing intermediate results via shared memory. Tools like Pydantic models enforce schema structure during extraction.

The architecture handles diverse document types (invoices, contracts, reports) by delegating subtasks dynamically. Agents use LLMs with custom tools for entity recognition and validation, but without guardrails, issues like inconsistent field mapping or fabricated data emerge. Vex callbacks intercept task outputs for schema compliance checks before handoff.

This setup scales to parallel extraction across document batches, with a Manager agent overseeing delegation and final aggregation.

risks.name: Agent delegation loops
risks.description: Agents endlessly pass incomplete extractions between roles, stalling progress
risks.mitigation: Vex task timeouts and delegation cycle detection

risks.name: Hallucinated tool outputs
risks.description: Agents fabricate missing fields with plausible but incorrect data
risks.mitigation: Vex schema compliance and field presence validation

risks.name: Task drift
risks.description: Agents reinterpret extraction schemas, missing required fields
risks.mitigation: Vex objective anchoring and completeness checks

risks.name: Inconsistent output quality
risks.description: Different agents produce varying JSON formats and accuracy levels
risks.mitigation: Vex type consistency and format standardization

implementation.title: Define Extraction Schema with Pydantic
implementation.description: Create structured output model for invoice data extraction

implementation.title: Configure CrewAI Extractor Agent
implementation.description: Set up specialized agent for schema-guided extraction
implementation.code:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew
from pydantic import BaseModel

extractor = Agent(
    role="Data Extractor",
    goal="Extract invoice data from documents using exact schema",
    backstory="Expert in parsing financial documents with 100% field accuracy",
    verbose=True,
    allow_delegation=False,
    output_pydantic=InvoiceSchema
)

extraction_task = Task(
    description="Extract invoice data from {document_text}",
    agent=extractor,
    context=["Use InvoiceSchema exactly"],
    output_pydantic=InvoiceSchema
)

implementation.title: Create Validation Agent
implementation.description: Second agent verifies extraction quality before final output
implementation.code:
validator = Agent(
    role="Quality Validator",
    goal="Verify extracted data completeness and accuracy",
    backstory="Meticulous auditor who rejects incomplete extractions",
    verbose=True,
    output_pydantic=dict  # Simple validation report
)

validation_task = Task(
    description="Validate {extracted_data} for schema compliance and accuracy",
    agent=validator,
    context=[extraction_task],
    expected_output="Validation report with pass/fail status"
)

implementation.title: Assemble and Execute Crew

vexIntegration.title: Install and Initialize Vex SDK
vexIntegration.description: Set up Vex for medium-risk data extraction verification
vexIntegration.code:
pip install crewai vex-sdk

from vex import Vex
from pydantic import BaseModel

# Initialize with your API key
vex = Vex(api_key="your-vex-api-key")

# Define verification spec for medium risk
extraction_spec = {
    "risk_profile": "medium",
    "verifications": ["schema_compliance", "field_accuracy", "completeness", "type_consistency"],
    "pydantic_model": InvoiceSchema
}

vexIntegration.title: Add Vex Callback to Extraction Task
vexIntegration.description: Intercept agent outputs for schema validation before delegation

vexIntegration.title: Vex Guardrails for Full Crew Pipeline
vexIntegration.description: Protect against delegation loops and output drift across all tasks
vexIntegration.code:
def crew_vex_guardrail(task, agent, output):
    # Check for loops (max 3 cycles per agent)
    cycles = getattr(task, '_cycle_count', 0)
    if cycles > 3:
        return vex.block("Delegation loop detected")
    task._cycle_count = cycles + 1
    
    # Schema + completeness verification
    result = vex.verify(
        data=output,
        spec=extraction_spec,
        safeguards={
            "prevent_loops": True,
            "anchor_objective": "invoice extraction",
            "output_consistency": True
        }
    )
    return result.valid

# Apply to crew
crew = Crew(
    agents=[extractor, validator],
    tasks=[extraction_task, validation_task],
    task_callback=crew_vex_guardrail
)

final_result = crew.kickoff(inputs={"document_text": document})

cta.heading: Secure Your CrewAI Data Extraction
cta.description: Add Vex guardrails today to ensure 100% schema-compliant extractions across your agent crews.
